title: Jitsi: Helm subchart integration and deployment
issueBody:
## Summary

Integrate [jitsi-helm](https://github.com/jitsi-contrib/jitsi-helm) as a Helm subchart into the krate chart. Support both in-cluster deployment and external Jitsi connection.

## Scope

1. Add jitsi-meet as Chart.yaml dependency with `condition: jitsi.install`
2. Add jitsi section to values.yaml - web, prosody (JWT auth), jicofo, jvb, jibri (optional recording)
3. Support external mode (`jitsi.external.enabled`) for existing Jitsi deployments
4. Add CRD YAML file: `charts/crds/jitsi-resources.yaml` with JitsiMeetProvider, JitsiMeetingTemplate, JitsiMeeting, JitsiRecording
5. Network policy for Jitsi media traffic (UDP 10000)
6. JWT secret management for programmatic room access
7. Verification: pods running, config.js accessible, health probe

## Reference

- [docs/jitsi/02-helm-deployment.md](packages/krate/docs/jitsi/02-helm-deployment.md) - full deployment spec
- [docs/jitsi/01-architecture.md](packages/krate/docs/jitsi/01-architecture.md) - system overview

## Dependencies

None - can start independently. Unblocks all other Jitsi work.
triageComment:
## Triage Summary
- **One-line**: Add Jitsi Meet as a Krate Helm chart deployment option, with in-cluster subchart support and external Jitsi connection mode.

## Critical Evaluation
- The reporter's analysis is accurate: this is not implemented yet, and the repository currently contains requirements documentation rather than chart resources.
- This is a feature/enabler rather than a bug. It is in scope for Krate infrastructure and unblocks the rest of the Jitsi issue chain.

## Root Cause Analysis
- Affected files:
  - `packages/krate/charts/Chart.yaml`
  - `packages/krate/charts/values.yaml`
  - `packages/krate/charts/templates/networkpolicy.yaml`
  - `packages/krate/charts/crds/jitsi-resources.yaml` (missing)
  - `packages/krate/docs/jitsi/02-helm-deployment.md`
  - `packages/krate/docs/jitsi/01-architecture.md`
- Actual root cause: the Jitsi Helm deployment spec was added as docs only. The Krate chart has not been updated with the `jitsi-meet` subchart dependency, values, CRDs, JWT secret wiring, or Jitsi-specific network policy/media handling.
- Code evidence: `Chart.yaml` currently lists `vela-core` and `kyverno` dependencies only; `values.yaml` has no `jitsi` section; `charts/crds/jitsi-resources.yaml` does not exist; the current chart network policy is generic and does not model JVB UDP 10000/media requirements.

## Affected Components
- Packages: krate, infrastructure
- Effort: effort:large

## Recommended Approach
Implement this in stages:
1. Add the `jitsi-meet` dependency to `packages/krate/charts/Chart.yaml` with `condition: jitsi.install` and an alias matching the deployment spec.
2. Add a `jitsi` section to `values.yaml` covering in-cluster install, external mode, web/prosody/jicofo/jvb/jibri overrides, JWT settings, webhook secret settings, and Krate-specific defaults.
3. Add `packages/krate/charts/crds/jitsi-resources.yaml` based on `packages/krate/docs/jitsi/03-crds-and-controllers.md`.
4. Add secret and network-policy templates for JWT/webhook secret management and JVB UDP 10000 media traffic.
5. Add Helm rendering/lint checks for both in-cluster and external modes.
6. Document deployment verification for pods running, `config.js` accessibility, and Krate/Jitsi health checks.

## Risk Analysis
- **Risk Level**: risk:high
- Helm dependency and packaging regressions: run `helm dependency build`, `helm lint`, and rendered manifest checks in CI for both `jitsi.install=true` and `jitsi.install=false`.
- JWT secret handling and auth misconfiguration: support `existingSecret` and generated-secret paths; never require secret literals in committed values; add template tests for JWT wiring.
- JVB UDP media path blocked in cluster networking: add explicit service/network-policy handling for UDP 10000 and document cloud/load-balancer requirements.
- In-cluster and external modes conflict: define clear precedence/validation for `jitsi.install` versus `jitsi.external.enabled`; add render tests for both modes.
- CRD schema mismatch with follow-up controller work: base CRDs directly on the Jitsi CRD/controller spec and coordinate field names with #624 before implementation.
qualityCommands: helm template krate packages/krate/charts -n krate-system --set jitsi.install=true --set argocd.enabled=false
helm template krate packages/krate/charts -n krate-system --set jitsi.install=false --set jitsi.external.enabled=true --set jitsi.external.url=https://meet.example.test --set argocd.enabled=false
